proposed_hypotheses.text: The 33 candidate features split into binary (15 features), 3-level (feature_001), and continuous (17 features). Knowing the type of each will determine which marginal tests apply.

analyses.code: for c in df.columns: print(c, df[c].nunique(), df[c].dtype)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each binary feature individually shifts mean pfs_months between feature=1 and feature=0 patients (two-sided t-test).
Mean pfs_months differs across the three levels of feature_001 (0 vs 1 vs 2), with monotonic gradient.
Each continuous feature is linearly correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r).

analyses.code: for c in bin_feats: stats.ttest_ind(y[df[c]==1], y[df[c]==0])
analyses.result_summary: Strongest binary effects (signed = mean(c=1) - mean(c=0)): feature_014 -1.352 mo (p~0), feature_027 +0.972 mo (p~0), feature_023 -0.327 mo (p=8.5e-56), feature_006 -0.305 mo (p=5.2e-46), feature_022 -0.228 mo (p=3.7e-6), feature_011 +0.221 mo (p=2.3e-4). Other binaries show |diff|<0.06 mo and p>0.08.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(y[df['feature_001']==0], y[df['feature_001']==1], y[df['feature_001']==2])
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotonic gradient. Mean pfs_months: feature_001=0 -> 5.27 (n=17,592); =1 -> 4.05 (n=24,971); =2 -> 2.91 (n=7,437). One-way ANOVA F=3528.9, p~0.

analyses.code: for c in cont_feats: stats.pearsonr(df[c], y)
analyses.result_summary: Dominant continuous predictor: feature_015 r=+0.766 (p~0). Secondary signals: feature_025 r=-0.123 (p=2.2e-167), feature_019 r=+0.102 (p=3.3e-115), feature_005 r=-0.038 (p=3.1e-17). Other continuous features have |r|<0.013 and p>0.004 — essentially uninformative as marginal linear predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all 33 features will reveal which marginal effects survive joint adjustment, distinguishing true predictors from features confounded by stronger correlates.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(y, sm.add_constant(df.drop(columns=['patient_id','pfs_months']))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.859. Significant adjusted coefficients (signed, on pfs_months scale): feature_015 +0.176/unit (t=454), feature_001 -1.171/level (t=-206), feature_014 -1.362 (t=-176), feature_027 +0.943 (t=98), feature_019 +0.469/unit (t=61), feature_025 -0.076/unit (t=-76), feature_023 -0.354 (t=-44), feature_006 -0.287 (t=-36), feature_022 -0.357 (t=-19), feature_005 -0.005/unit (t=-21), feature_021 -0.0004/unit (t=-9). All other features have |t|<2 and p>0.05 — they are noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is the candidate treatment variable: (a) its prevalence (~20%) and binary nature are treatment-like, (b) its adjusted coefficient (+0.94 mo on pfs_months) is the only large positive binary effect, and (c) if it is treatment-assigned by design, baseline covariates should be balanced between feature_027=0 and =1 groups.

analyses.code: for each covariate c: t-test (continuous) or chi-square (binary) of c by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Across all 32 non-outcome covariates, no test reaches p<0.05 — every difference between feature_027=0 and =1 is consistent with random assignment (smallest p observed was p=0.124 for feature_001, with rates 78.6% vs 80.0%). This is consistent with a randomized or pseudo-randomized treatment assignment for feature_027.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_027 as treatment, the marginal (unadjusted) treatment effect on pfs_months is positive: mean pfs_months is higher in feature_027=1 than =0.

analyses.code: y[df['feature_027']==1].mean() - y[df['feature_027']==0].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 5.089 in feature_027=1 (n=10,022) vs 4.117 in feature_027=0 (n=39,978). Marginal TE = +0.972 mo, t-test p~0. Adjusted TE in the full multivariable model is +0.943 mo (essentially identical to unadjusted, consistent with randomization).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The marginal treatment effect of feature_027 (+0.97 mo) is not constant across the cohort: at least one feature interacts with feature_027 such that the treatment effect is meaningfully larger or smaller within a subgroup.

analyses.code: for each c: OLS(y ~ controls + c + T + T*c)
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen (controlling for top prognostics) ranked by p-value: feature_006 int=-1.461 p~0 (TE=+1.45 at c=0 vs ~0 at c=1); feature_023 int=-1.635 p~0 (TE=+1.63 at c=0 vs ~0 at c=1); feature_005 int=-0.026/unit p~0 (TE shrinks across the range); feature_022 int=-0.987 p=2e-102 (TE=+0.99 at c=0 vs ~0 at c=1); feature_011 int=+0.887 p=8e-57 (TE=+0.92 at c=0 vs +1.80 at c=1). All other interactions are |coef|<0.1 and p>1e-3 (likely artifactual).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The presence of feature_006=1 nullifies the treatment effect of feature_027: TE is approximately zero in feature_006=1 patients but strongly positive in feature_006=0 patients.
The presence of feature_023=1 nullifies the treatment effect of feature_027: TE is approximately zero in feature_023=1 patients but strongly positive in feature_023=0 patients.
The presence of feature_022=1 nullifies the treatment effect of feature_027: TE is approximately zero in feature_022=1 patients but positive in feature_022=0 patients.

analyses.code: two-sample t-test of pfs_months by feature_027 within each stratum of feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0: mean(T=1)=5.601 vs mean(T=0)=4.121, TE=+1.480, p~0. feature_006=1: mean(T=1)=4.135 vs mean(T=0)=4.109, TE=+0.026, p=0.52. The treatment effect is fully nullified by feature_006=1.

analyses.code: stratified TE by feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: mean(T=1)=5.773 vs mean(T=0)=4.117, TE=+1.656, p~0. feature_023=1: mean(T=1)=4.143 vs mean(T=0)=4.116, TE=+0.027, p=0.45. Treatment effect fully nullified by feature_023=1.

analyses.code: stratified TE by feature_022
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=0: TE=+1.027, p~0. feature_022=1: TE=-0.156, p=0.15. Treatment effect nullified (and possibly slightly reversed) by feature_022=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1 is INDEPENDENTLY sufficient to nullify the feature_027 treatment effect: in patients with exactly one nullifier set (and the other two zero), TE remains ~0.

analyses.code: stratified TE within each single-positive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 alone (023=0,022=0): TE=+0.083, p=0.12. feature_023=1 alone (006=0,022=0): TE=+0.061, p=0.17. feature_022=1 alone (006=0,023=0): TE=-0.204, p=0.13. Each single nullifier independently abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 (call this fav3), the unadjusted treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is substantially larger than the marginal +0.97 mo seen in the whole cohort.
In the complement (feature_006=1 OR feature_023=1 OR feature_022=1), the treatment effect is approximately zero.

analyses.code: TE in fav3
analyses.result_summary: fav3 (n=17,372 = 35% of cohort): mean(T=1)=6.876 vs mean(T=0)=4.117, TE=+2.758, p~0 (n_T=3461 vs n_C=13911). The TE in fav3 is 2.85x the marginal TE.

analyses.code: TE in complement of fav3
analyses.result_summary: Complement (n=32,628): TE=+0.030, p=0.29. The treatment effect is effectively zero in any patient with at least one of feature_006/023/022 positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within fav3, residual heterogeneity in TE is dominated by the continuous biomarker feature_005: TE shrinks as feature_005 increases.

analyses.code: OLS within fav3: y ~ controls + feature_005 + T + T*feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Within fav3, the only feature with a meaningfully large T x feature interaction is feature_005 (int=-0.064/unit, p~0). The next-strongest interactions (e.g., feature_031 int=+0.20, feature_020 int=+0.33) imply TE shifts of <0.4 mo and look like noise relative to feature_005's +5 mo signal across its useful range. Quintiles of feature_005 within fav3: Q1 (<=1.61) TE=+5.02; Q2 (1.61-3.27) TE=+5.06; Q3 (3.27-6.02) TE=+3.45; Q4 (6.02-12.18) TE=-0.01; Q5 (>12.18) TE=-0.02. Suggests a sharp threshold rather than a linear interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within fav3, the treatment effect of feature_027 is large and positive when feature_005<=5 and is essentially zero when feature_005>5, indicating feature_005 has a threshold-like nullifying effect at ~5.

analyses.code: TE in fav3 split by 1-unit bins of feature_005

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete responder subgroup for feature_027 is fav4 = {feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5}. Inside fav4, TE on pfs_months is approximately +5.0 months. Outside fav4, TE is approximately 0. This single 4-feature rule captures essentially all of the heterogeneity in the treatment effect.

analyses.code: stratified TE in fav4 vs complement; full-cohort OLS with subgroup interaction
analyses.result_summary: fav4 (n=9338, 18.7% of cohort): mean(T=1)=8.998 vs mean(T=0)=3.984 (calculated from sums), unadjusted TE=+5.014, p~0. Outside fav4 (n=40,662): TE=+0.022 (combination of fav3-but-feature_005>5 with TE=-0.002 and any-binary-nullifier with TE=+0.030). Full-cohort interaction OLS y ~ prognostics + four nullifiers + T + fav4 + T*fav4: T main coefficient -0.004 (p=0.11), T*fav4 coefficient = +4.982 (p~0). The interaction model says: outside fav4, treatment effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero; inside fav4, treatment effect is +4.98 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, and feature_005 (cutpoint 5) are PURE treatment-effect modifiers — they have no prognostic main effect on pfs_months in untreated patients.

analyses.code: in subset feature_027==0, mean pfs_months by each modifier
analyses.result_summary: Among untreated (feature_027=0) patients, mean pfs_months by modifier: feature_006=1: 4.109 vs =0: 4.121 (diff -0.012); feature_023=1: 4.116 vs =0: 4.117 (diff -0.001); feature_022=1: 4.126 vs =0: 4.116 (diff +0.010); feature_005>5: 4.122 vs <=5: 4.112 (diff +0.010). All four 'nullifiers' are prognostically inert. They are pure interaction-only / predictive (not prognostic) modifiers — characteristic of biomarkers of treatment resistance/sensitivity rather than disease severity. In the full-cohort interaction OLS (Iter 12), the main effects of feature_006, 023, 022, and feature_005 all have p>=0.24 once T*fav4 is in the model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1 enhances the treatment effect of feature_027 on top of the fav4 subgroup (so true responder subgroup is fav4 AND feature_011=1).

analyses.code: stratified TE within fav4 by feature_011
analyses.result_summary: Within fav4, TE in feature_011=0 = +5.001 vs feature_011=1 = +5.235 (n_011=1 in fav4 is small). Difference is 0.23 mo and not significant (T x feature_011 interaction within fav4: p=0.31). The earlier whole-cohort apparent enhancement by feature_011 (TE +0.92 in 011=0, +1.80 in 011=1) was driven by feature_011=1 patients more often falling in fav3 with feature_005<=5 — once we condition on fav4, feature_011 adds nothing meaningful. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No additional binary feature among {feature_002, 010, 012, 016, 018, 020, 024, 028, 031} modifies the treatment effect within fav4 by more than +/-0.3 months.

analyses.code: stratified TE within fav4 by each remaining binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Within fav4, TE by binary feature: feature_031 (TE@0=+4.97, @1=+5.06); feature_016 (+5.00 vs +5.27); feature_028 (+5.02 vs +4.69); feature_002 (+5.01 vs +4.90); feature_024 (+4.99 vs +5.06); feature_010 (+5.04 vs +4.99); feature_012 (+4.99 vs +5.15); feature_018 (+5.01 vs +5.08); feature_020 (+5.04 vs +4.76). All differences are within +/-0.3 mo and consistent with sampling noise; no further binary modifier is identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 is itself a competing treatment (i.e., has an independent positive main effect on pfs_months when adjusted for feature_027 and the prognostics).

analyses.code: OLS y ~ prognostics + feature_006 + feature_023 + feature_022 + feature_011 + feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_011 = +0.0213 mo, p=0.36. Not a competing treatment — feature_011 has no independent main effect on pfs_months once feature_027 is included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level prognostic) is also a treatment-effect modifier within fav4 — patients with feature_001=2 (worst prognosis) derive less treatment benefit than feature_001=0/1.

analyses.code: stratified TE within fav4 by level of feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Within fav4, TE by feature_001 level: 0 -> +4.870 (n=3330); 1 -> +5.091 (n=4606); 2 -> +4.812 (n=1402). Differences are within ~0.3 mo; T x feature_001 interaction within fav4 is statistically significant (p=1.7e-6) but trivially small in magnitude (interaction coef -0.030 per level). Hypothesis weakly supported: there IS a small TE attenuation in higher feature_001 levels, but it is clinically negligible relative to the ~+5 mo treatment effect itself.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The dominant prognostic predictors of pfs_months independent of feature_027 treatment status are: feature_015 (positive, ~+0.18 mo per unit; range 30-90), feature_001 (negative, ~-1.17 mo per level), feature_014 (negative, ~-1.37 mo for =1), feature_019 (positive, ~+0.49 mo per unit; range 1.5-5.5), and feature_025 (negative, ~-0.08 mo per unit; range 0-25).

analyses.code: OLS y ~ feature_015 + feature_001 + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025 + feature_027 within fav4 (and similar for full cohort)
analyses.result_summary: Within fav4 OLS: feature_015 +0.177 (t=1011); feature_001 -1.176 (t=-460); feature_014 -1.378 (t=-393); feature_019 +0.488 (t=140); feature_025 -0.079 (t=-178); feature_027 +4.978 (t=1153). Effective R^2 within fav4 is very high. These five prognostic features fully explain the baseline (untreated) PFS trajectory; feature_027 then adds a near-uniform +4.98 mo on top in fav4. None of the prognostic features modify the TE in any clinically meaningful way (largest interaction is T x feature_015 with coef +0.0053/unit -> +/-0.04 mo across the feature_015 IQR).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There are no additional clinically meaningful treatment-effect modifiers within fav4: TE is approximately uniform across all measured features once we condition on the four-feature subgroup definition.

analyses.code: for each c in remaining features: TE in fav4 by c (binary) or by IQR (continuous)
analyses.result_summary: Across all 28 non-defining features, the largest residual T x feature interaction within fav4 has |coef| < 0.04 mo across the relevant feature range. Several reach nominal statistical significance (e.g., T x feature_015 p=3e-36, T x feature_001 p=2e-6) due to the very large fav4 sample, but the implied TE varies only between +4.94 and +5.01 mo across the relevant range — clinically negligible and likely an artifact of left-truncation at pfs_months=0. Conclusion: the four-feature rule fav4 is sufficient to characterize treatment-effect heterogeneity; no additional refinement is warranted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is concentrated entirely in the subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5 (~19% of the cohort), where the adjusted treatment effect is +4.98 months. Outside this subgroup, the treatment effect is statistically and clinically indistinguishable from zero. Each of the four defining features is a pure (predictive, non-prognostic) treatment-effect modifier; any single positive nullifier (feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, or feature_005>5) is sufficient to abolish the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: see iterations 7-13 and 18-19
analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: TE inside fav4 (adjusted, OLS with prognostic controls) = +4.98 mo (p~0); TE outside fav4 = -0.004 mo (p=0.11) per the full-cohort interaction model. The four nullifiers each independently abolish the effect (TE in single-positive subgroups: feature_006=1 alone +0.08, feature_023=1 alone +0.06, feature_022=1 alone -0.20, feature_005>5 within fav3 -0.00). Subgroup definition is tight, additive in its nullifiers, and interpretable.
